scores.materiality_reasoning: Priesthood is a social role with symbolic and sometimes economic outcomes like subsistence and status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Eligibility for priesthood is a broad category of religious and civic office.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Aristotle describes this as a retirement role held for the remainder of life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group is defined by age ('elderly'), which is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle describes this as part of the structural planning of the Greek polis.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The 'should' phrasing leans toward normative idealizing, but describes a standard Greek institutional practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being elderly does not inherently define one as a priest.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Ownership of land is a primary tangible material resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Land ownership is a foundational societal axis and primary form of wealth.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Exclusion from land ownership was a lifelong structural condition of slavery.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a legal birth-status, requiring manumission to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the standard economic structure of the contemporary Greek world.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a manifest necessity of the social system.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slaves were not by definition excluded from all forms of possession, only structural land ownership here.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to deliberate on war is a concrete legal right with massive material consequences (war/peace).
scores.generality_reasoning: Restricted to a specific procedural context: wars against neighboring states.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds as long as the citizen remains a landowner in the frontier region.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Landowner location can change, but represents a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Explicitly cites existing laws ('some people have a law').
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes an existing legal fact found in certain polities.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Frontier residency does not inherently mean loss of voting rights.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Water is a physical good essential for subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to utilities/water supply is a broad category of infrastructure rights.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Provision is permanent urban infrastructure for the body of citizens.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary urban planning and siege strategies.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a physical mode of supply invented for practical use.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not technically define water access.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from physical disaster (death/wounds) is a bodily material resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis of security and preservation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The protection of city walls is a permanent structural feature.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a stable structural category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses standard military/urban practices in Greek states.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Framed as a necessary recommendation for state survival.
scores.tautology_reasoning: To be a citizen does not mean one is physically inside walls.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to the agora has direct material effects on commerce and civic standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific institution/space (the 'free agora').
scores.persistence_reasoning: The exclusion is a permanent feature of the social class's role.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Farmer/Artisan status is a durable occupational identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites the custom in Thessaly as a contemporary example.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports specific laws and customs practiced in certain regions.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a farmer does not inherently exclude one from central squares.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to the gymnasium is participation in a social/educational practice.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific educational institution.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the life stage of youth vs. old age.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age (being a youth/minor) is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes standard Greek social organization of training.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Mixture of describing 'institutions' and saying it 'would give amenity'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Minors are not by definition excluded from all gymnasia.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for magistracy is a concrete legal right to political power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational access to political power and government.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the life stage of minority/youth.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Minor status is biologically fixed by age.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the standard constitutional structure of most Greek poleis.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Presented as a natural and standard distinction observed in cities.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a minor is not defined by lack of magistracy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Sharing in government is a concrete right with direct material influence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis of political participation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Citizenship rights were generally held for life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a stable structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the contemporary state of affairs in the author's city/class.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Explicit statement of fact ('in our state all the citizens share...').
scores.tautology_reasoning: In Aristotle's definition, the citizen *is* the one who shares in ruling.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to types of education has social/outcomes-based value.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad educational policy across different life stages.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Attached to specific developmental phases (childhood).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being a child is a biologically fixed age state.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Based on Aristotle's observations of contemporary developmental psychology.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Framed as a 'necessary' philosophical and pedagogical logic.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Minors are not defined by the priority of body training.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to reproductive timing relates to bodily functions and family structure.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad marital/family regulation with demographic impact.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The timing of marriage sets the course of the remainder of life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Spousal status is durable once entered.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses duty of the contemporary 'lawgiver'.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Very normative: 'the legislator should pay attention'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Sychronized timing is not part of the definition of a spouse.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Leisure is an abstract state of being (though it requires material means).
scores.generality_reasoning: Leisure is a broad class of life outcome in Greek thought.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The lack of leisure is a lifelong structural condition of slavery.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the current social reality of the Greek world.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a proverb as a known and accepted social axiom.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Enslavement is being property, not necessarily the count of spare time.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Holding office is a concrete legal right and social role.
scores.generality_reasoning: Fundamental access to political power and rule.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Part of a lifelong civic progression.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is stable and structural.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Central feature of contemporary Greek civic life.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Links existing roles to normative courses of training.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not technically define training programs.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Regulation of marriage age has huge impact on life chances and health.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the timing of one life event (marriage).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong impact on health, offspring, and status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Minors defined by biologically fixed age.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Part ofcontemporary legalistic/societal regulation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Prescriptive: 'it is fitting for...', 'at thirty-seven...'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Minors are not defined as being unable to marry at a specific age.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to life is the ultimate bodily entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: The most foundational societal outcome possible.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Death is permanent.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Physical deformity at birth is immutable and biological.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects common Greek practices (e.g., Sparta).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Commands a specific law based on 'regular customs'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Deformed infants are not defined by the law to expose them.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from procreative duty affects bodily autonomy and role.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a particular civic obligation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Covers the 'rest of their lives'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Elderly status is biologically fixed by age.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes age-based regulation within the polis.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Imperative framing: 'must be discharged'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Elderly people are not defined by procreative retirement.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Public meals provide subsistence and essential civic status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific institution: syssitia (public meals).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Exclusion lasts until the specific age of promotion.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Minor status is biologically fixed by age.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary Spartan/Cretan style institutions.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a specific group description ('not yet promoted').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Minors are not by definition excluded from the institution of meals.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to public performance is a role-based participation right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific type of cultural event.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Duration of childhood/adolescence.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Minor status is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects standard cultural regulations of the time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: A mix of standard rule and reasoning for education.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Minors are not defined as being banned from comedy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from beating is a direct bodily protection.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a procedural punishment for certain acts.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The status of being beatable ends upon adulthood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Minor status is biologically age-fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary disciplinary and legal codes.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the legal penalty 'must be punished' for a found act.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a minor does not inherently imply corporal punishment.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Education is access to a social role/practice with material impact.
scores.generality_reasoning: A broad foundational system for all citizens.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Public education is a lifelong structural foundation.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is stable structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Explicitly cites the Spartans as a current example.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Praises contemporary historical examples while arguing for its necessity.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not define the system of education used.
